stem: In triangle ABC, angle C is a right angle. Point D lies on segment AB, and point E lies on segment AC such that segment DE is parallel to segment BC. The length of BC is 60 units, the length of DE is 5 units, and the area of triangle ABC is 450 square units. What is the length, in units, of segment EC?
rationale: Since ∠C is right, the area of △ABC is (1/2)·AC·BC = 450. With BC = 60, this gives AC = 15. Because DE ∥ BC, triangle ADE is similar to triangle ABC, so DE/BC = AE/AC, i.e., 5/60 = AE/15, giving AE = 15/12 = 5/4. Then EC = AC − AE = 15 − 5/4 = 55/4 = 13.75.
